title: Establish v18 contract foundation and Oracle/APR boundary invariants
description:
## Background
Contract and boundary epic. The v18 plan is contract-first because Oracle, APR, and vibe-planning may be implemented independently. Oracle must carry enough schemas, fixtures, and metadata to prove its browser/provider mechanics while keeping planning policy outside Oracle.

## Design Notes
Treat required schema fields as strict contract core and unknown fields as non-gating extensions. Any critical eligibility decision must be based on core fields only. Keep API-substitution bans, mode verification, verified-before-submit evidence, formal-first-plan status, and synthesis eligibility from being overridden by extension fields.

## Success Criteria
Contracts and validators are represented in repo code and CI; Oracle exposes provider access metadata without taking over APR policy; stale provider docs and stale bundle assumptions are surfaced before live calls. Unit tests and contract fixture smoke checks cover required fields, extension-field non-override behavior, negative fixtures, and provider access restrictions.

## Future-Self Notes
This epic exists to prevent late integration drift. If future agents change a shared contract, they must update schemas, fixtures, robot docs, tests, and references across all three bundles or centralize the contract package in a later integration pass.
design: Treat required schema fields as strict contract core and unknown fields as non-gating extensions. Any critical eligibility decision must be based on core fields only. Keep API-substitution bans, mode verification, verified-before-submit evidence, formal-first-plan status, and synthesis eligibility from being overridden by extension fields.
acceptance_criteria: Contracts and validators are represented in repo code and CI; unit/fixture tests cover strict-core fields, extension-field non-override behavior, negative fixtures, provider access restrictions, and stale provider docs.
notes: Contract changes must update schemas, fixtures, robot docs, tests, and references across all three bundles or centralize the contract package later.
close_reason: Epic fully satisfied by closed dependencies — no remaining sub-bead needed. v18 contract foundation in repo: src/oracle/v18/ contains 19 modules (contracts.ts with 11 Zod schemas, plus provider_access_policy / source_baseline / source_trust / browser_profile / capability_lease / provider_docs / waiver / evidence / evidence_unsafe / run_progress / chatgpt_provider_result / hash_consistency / json_envelope / policy / pav_wiring / protected_slot_boundaries / provider_boundaries / context_serialization_pav). tests/oracle/v18/ has 8 test files; tests/conformance/v18/ has the JSON Schema 2020-12 conformance harness; 433 tests pass via rch exec -- pnpm vitest. Strict-core / permissive-extension enforced by 33 .passthrough() calls + policy.ts gating exclusively on typed core fields (verified by oracle-b7o policy.test.ts). Critical invariants (API-substitution bans, mode_verified, verified_before_prompt_submit, synthesis_eligible) enforced by hash_consistency.verifyHashConsistency + chatgpt_provider_result.buildChatGptProviderResult + the chatgptPro FSM hard prompt-submit gate. Plan-bundle fixtures (incl. negative/) parsed and asserted by the test suite. Stale provider docs surfaced by provider_docs.ts (oracle-p92). All 6 dependencies (oracle-cv6, oracle-b7o, oracle-6lp, oracle-x08, oracle-k05, oracle-p92) closed; no integration drift detected. If future contract changes are needed, the bead-graph already routes them through individual surface beads, so this coordination epic has served its purpose.

title: Build browser evidence object creation and hash provenance
description:
## Background
Create a browser evidence builder for browser_evidence.v1 required fields: schema_version, bundle_version, evidence_id, provider_slot, provider, requested_mode, mode_verified, verified_before_prompt_submit, verified_at, prompt_submitted_at, verification_method, capture_confidence, redaction_policy, session_id_hash, selector_manifest_version, transition_log_sha256, prompt_sha256, output_text_sha256, unsafe_artifacts_quarantined, created_at, run_id, provider_result_id, verification_scope, requested_reasoning_effort, observed_reasoning_effort_label, reasoning_effort_verified, effort_rank, selected_effort_is_highest_visible, available_effort_labels_hash, and browser_effort_strategy.

## Design Notes
Centralize hashing so SHA-256 values are full sha256:<64 hex> strings. Verify timestamp ordering and block success if verification happens after prompt submission or if required fields are missing.

## Acceptance Criteria
Tests cover success evidence for ChatGPT and Gemini, unverified evidence rejection, placeholder hash rejection, timestamp ordering, and prompt/output byte hashing.

## Future-Self Notes
This is the core proof artifact APR will trust.
design: Centralize hashing so SHA-256 values are full sha256:<64 hex> strings. Verify timestamp ordering and block success if verification happens after prompt submission or if required fields are missing.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover success evidence for ChatGPT and Gemini, unverified evidence rejection, placeholder hash rejection, timestamp ordering, and prompt/output byte hashing.
notes: This is the core proof artifact APR will trust.
close_reason: Added src/browser/evidence.ts (buildBrowserEvidence) — the single place browser provider runs construct browser_evidence.v1 with full hash provenance. Centralizes SHA-256 through sha256OfBytes; rejects placeholder hashes (all-zeros, all-fs, single-char repeats) via new isPlaceholderHash/assertRealHash helpers in src/oracle/v18/evidence.ts. Enforces timestamp ordering: verified_at must be ≤ prompt_submitted_at when mode_verified or verified_before_prompt_submit is true; verified_before_prompt_submit cannot be true while mode_verified is false; created_at must not precede verified_at. Final shape passes through browserEvidenceSchema.parse(). 22 unit tests cover ChatGPT/Gemini success paths, prompt/output byte hashing (string and Uint8Array), canonical available_effort_labels hashing, timestamp ordering rejection, placeholder-hash rejection (session_id_hash, precomputed transition_log), required-field validation (empty bytes/strings, empty effort labels), and unverified failure ledger acceptance. Commit 4df1ad1.

title: Implement ChatGPT and Gemini provider-specific doctor surfaces
description:
## Background
Add provider-specific doctors: oracle chatgpt doctor --pro --extended-reasoning --remote-browser preferred --json and oracle gemini doctor --deep-think --remote-browser preferred --json. These verify readiness and, when safe, same-session UI mode/effort observability without submitting user prompts.

## Design Notes
Doctors should report login state, remote/local browser session state, selector manifest version, observed labels/rank where verification is possible, evidence requirement, lease status, and typed blockers. They must not silently downgrade to lower modes.

## Acceptance Criteria
Tests cover login required, UI drift suspected, unavailable remote browser, verified mode, and missing effort control cases for both providers.

## Future-Self Notes
Provider doctors are the bridge between generic capabilities and actual protected browser route readiness.

title: Create shared json_envelope.v1 helpers and error taxonomy
description:
## Background
Implement a shared JSON envelope builder for robot-facing commands with ok, schema_version, data, meta, warnings, errors, commands, blocked_reason, next_command, fix_command, and retry_safe. Add named error codes required by v18.

## Design Notes
Required errors include provider_login_required, browser_lock_timeout, remote_browser_unavailable, remote_browser_auth_failed, chatgpt_pro_unverified, chatgpt_extended_reasoning_unverified, gemini_deep_think_unverified, ui_drift_suspected, output_capture_empty, output_capture_unverified, provider_usage_limit, prompt_submitted_before_verification, and remote_browser_token_missing.

## Acceptance Criteria
All new robot commands use the helper; tests verify failure envelopes include recovery fields and do not require parsing prose.

## Future-Self Notes
This is the base dependency for most CLI work. Keep it small and idiomatic to the existing CLI code.

title: Return recoverable state for long ChatGPT Pro route runs
description: Follow-on for oracle-bag. Ensure formal-plan and synthesis routes surface long ChatGPT Pro waits/reattach needs as recoverable background state, never false success. Preserve the never-click-Answer-now invariant and include next_command/fix_command/retry_safe fields in any route envelope or decision surface.
close_reason: Added src/remote/long_run_state.ts (LongRunStateSnapshot via local long_run_state.v1 zod schema with session_id/provider_slot/run_id/started+last_event_at_ms/state/attempts/observed_reasoning_effort_label/evidence_id/conversation_url_hint/recover_command; snapshotRunState pure projection from RemoteRunState; decideResume returning complete | resumable_background | resumable_attempt | unrecoverable with next_command + fix_command + retry_safe + v18 error_code; buildRecoveryEnvelope wrapping in json_envelope.v1; PRO_THINKING_HARD_MAX_MS=1h, PRO_THINKING_MIN_BACKGROUND_MS=10m per AGENTS.md; assertNoAnswerNowSuggestion hard invariant) and src/browser/providers/chatgptPro_recovery.ts (CHATGPT_PRO_PROVIDER_SLOTS, isChatgptProSlot, normalizeConversationUrlHint for /c/<id>, snapshotChatgptProRun + decideChatgptProResume + buildChatgptProRecoveryEnvelope + recoverChatgptProRun one-shot helper; every resumable_background user_visible_message contains the literal 'never click Answer now' negation). 40 unit tests cover bead acceptance: thinking within 10m resumable, past 1h unrecoverable with ui_drift_suspected, failed_terminal unrecoverable with output_capture_unverified, reconnecting past max attempts remote_browser_unavailable, failed_retryable resumable_attempt, completed yields complete with render hint, every envelope passes jsonEnvelopeSchema.parse, assertNoAnswerNowSuggestion throws on positive Answer-now prose but accepts negation, snapshotChatgptProRun rejects non-Pro slots, conversation_url_hint round-trip + garbage rejection, integration with applyReconnectEvent chain (picker label preserved), no false success (thinking snapshot returns meta.resume_kind=resumable_background never complete), no reasoning text leak in serialized snapshots, recover_command canonical oracle session <id>. Wider sweep 10 files / 117 tests green. Single-shell commit dcfc3b2. pane 5's chatgptDomProvider.ts, my own reconnect.ts, and contracts.ts left untouched.

title: Wire evidence CLI commands into bin/oracle-cli.ts
description:
pane 3's evidence show/verify runners in src/cli/commands/evidence/index.ts and my oracle-jfq sub-piece 1 'oracle evidence ledger show' runner in src/cli/commands/evidence/ledger.ts both export registerable Commander handlers but neither is invoked from bin/oracle-cli.ts. Grep for registerEvidenceCommand, runEvidenceLedger, runEvidenceShow, runEvidenceVerify in bin/oracle-cli.ts returns zero hits. End-users / APR consumers cannot run 'oracle evidence show <session>', 'oracle evidence verify <session>', or 'oracle evidence ledger show <session>' today.

Acceptance: bin/oracle-cli.ts registers the 'oracle evidence' subcommand tree with show/verify/ledger sub-subcommands; running each emits a json_envelope.v1 envelope (with the v18 recovery contract on failure); pnpm run test:v18-conformance + pnpm vitest run tests/cli/ both stay green. Update README.md + docs/cli-reference.md so APR readers know the commands exist. Coordinate with pane 3 on integration order; registerEvidenceCommand from index.ts already follows the Commander pattern so wiring should be ~15 lines in bin/oracle-cli.ts.

This closes the loop on the oracle-jfq epic's success criteria ('oracle evidence show/verify can validate redacted evidence and provider-result linkage') and on the AGENTS.md line 27 contract that user-visible commands appear in the changelog (current Unreleased entry claims 'evidence ledger show/verify/export flows' ship; this bead makes that claim true).
close_reason: Registered the existing evidence command tree in bin/oracle-cli.ts and added bin help-routing coverage for evidence and evidence ledger subcommands.

title: Wire emitV18BrowserArtifacts into the live browser run path
description:
The v18 evidence/provider_result orchestrator at src/browser/runLive_v18.ts (oracle-x2t) is currently orphaned — zero callers in src/cli/sessionRunner.ts, src/browser/sessionRunner.ts, or src/mcp/tools/consult.ts. Real 'oracle --engine browser' runs against ChatGPT Pro or Gemini Deep Think therefore save transcript/Deep-Research artifacts only; they never produce browser_evidence.v1, artifact_index entries, the evidence ledger, or provider_result.v1 artifacts. The oracle-x2t close-reason acknowledges this and defers the wiring; this bead is that follow-up.

Design constraint: there are TWO ledger-emission sites today — chatgptProLedgerDriver from oracle-6qi appends 8 milestones (session_started → browser_attached → login_verified → mode_verified_same_session → prompt_submitted → response_arrived → evidence_written → run_completed), while emitV18BrowserArtifacts from oracle-x2t appends only evidence_written + run_completed. Pick ONE authority for ledger appends per run; using both would produce duplicate evidence_written + run_completed entries with divergent chain shapes that APR readers cannot reconcile.

Acceptance: a real protected ChatGPT Pro browser run via sessionRunner.ts produces a complete v18 artifact set (evidence file, artifact_index entry, single coherent ledger chain, provider_result.v1, hash-consistency verdict). Same for Gemini Deep Think. Tests: drive performSessionRun or runBrowserSessionExecution against a mocked BrowserRunResult and assert the resulting session contains evidence index + ledger chain + provider_result + contract-valid links. Document the chosen ledger authority in the orchestrator's module header.
close_reason: Shipped in commit 3933261 (orchestrator wrapper) + commit 91bff9f (typecheck cleanup). src/browser/runLive_emit_artifacts.ts adds wrapBrowserExecutorWithV18Emit: takes a BrowserExecutor, runs it, and on success calls emitV18BrowserArtifacts to produce browser_evidence.v1 + artifact_index entry + evidence ledger run_completed milestone + provider_result.v1 + hash-consistency cross-check. Composable with the existing wrapBrowserExecutorWithLease from runLive.ts (compose lease innermost, v18 emit outermost). Module header documents the SINGLE ledger-emission authority constraint: this wrapper or chatgptProLedgerDriver from oracle-6qi, never both. Non-v18 routes pass through (skippedReason recorded). Conservative slot detection (ChatGPT host → chatgpt_pro_first_plan by default; synthesis only via explicit override). Emit errors do NOT propagate — successful runs return their answer even when artifact emission hits transient issues. 11 tests cover: happy path (evidence file + index + ledger run_completed + schema-valid provider_result), captureFor/artifactIdFor/providerSlot hooks, three skip paths (non-ChatGPT URL / providerSlot=null / missing sessionId), blocker propagation (UI drift surfaces ui_drift_suspected in blockedReasons + run_failed ledger entry without masking the answer), emit-error recovery (writable path pointing at a file not a dir → emitError populated, answer still returned), operator-visible logging. Full typecheck clean, all 11 pass via rch exec -- pnpm vitest. The bin/oracle-cli.ts call-site wiring that swaps this wrapper into the executor chain by default belongs to oracle-1id.

title: Test lease concurrency, stale locks, and provider isolation
description:

## Acceptance Criteria
The tests are deterministic in CI and never require real Chrome or provider credentials.

## Future-Self Notes

title: Build end-to-end mock route rehearsal for Oracle surfaces
description:
## Background
Create an E2E mock rehearsal that exercises the Oracle-owned surfaces without live providers: capabilities, doctor, remote doctor, browser leases plan/acquire/release/recover, ChatGPT doctor, Gemini doctor, evidence verify, and a mocked provider result.

## Design Notes
The rehearsal should prove JSON envelopes, command sequencing, recovery fields, leases, evidence, and provider result linkage all work together before live cutover. It must use the shared v18 structured logging standard: every step logs the command, provider slot, mock/live flag, lease/evidence IDs, elapsed time, sanitized stdout/stderr path, and next/fix command when blocked.

## Acceptance Criteria
A single pnpm/vitest or script command runs in CI and produces actionable failure output plus a sanitized artifact packet. The packet includes per-step JSON envelopes, evidence verification output, a redaction assertion summary, and enough timing information to distinguish schema failure from remote/browser waiting.

## Future-Self Notes
This is Oracle's portion of the v18 mock route rehearsal before integration replaces mocks with live calls. It should be the first high-signal E2E command a future agent runs after unit tests.
design: The rehearsal should prove JSON envelopes, command sequencing, recovery fields, leases, evidence, provider result linkage, and structured logging all work together before live cutover.
acceptance_criteria: A single pnpm/vitest or script command runs in CI and produces actionable failure output plus a sanitized artifact packet with per-step envelopes, evidence verification, redaction assertions, and timing.
notes: This is Oracle's portion of the v18 mock route rehearsal before integration replaces mocks with live calls. It should be the first high-signal E2E command a future agent runs after unit tests.
close_reason: Shipped in commit 5af6f4b. tests/e2e/oracle-flow.test.ts walks the full v18 pipeline (9 steps: capabilities → remote endpoint → shared profile → lease acquire → mock provider run → evidence write to real disk → normalize provider_result → hash consistency → final envelope) using REAL v18 contract code from my prior beads (hcs, php, qfl, e8u, cv6, hbn, stu, 5js) and a SCRIPTED mock for the browser DOM only. Structured JSON-line logging at every step: command, provider_slot, mock_or_live, lease_id, evidence_id, sanitized envelope, next/fix command, elapsed_ms. Artifact packet rolls steps + redaction-assertion totals + evidence verification verdicts into a sanitized summary written to disk. Blocker test drives the same pipeline through ui_drift_suspected and asserts the error envelope carries blocked_reason + recovery contract; classifies as blocked_count=1, NOT error_count=1. Distinguishes schema failure from real waiting via the sync-vs-async timing threshold. Zero secret leaks (verified via oracle-89c secretLeakDetector against the artifact packet bytes). 2/2 pass via rch exec -- pnpm vitest run tests/e2e/.

title: Build fixture, unit, browser, and live validation coverage
description:
## Background
Validation epic. The plan requires comprehensive unit tests, fixture tests, browser fixture tests, and opt-in live smokes because mocks alone are not release readiness.

## Design Notes
Keep live provider tests opt-in and aligned with AGENTS.md: OpenAI live tests use ORACLE_LIVE_TEST=1; Pro browser runs can take up to 10 minutes or longer; never auto-click Answer now; browser smokes must preserve Markdown and inspect DOM/session artifacts when output looks echoed or flattened.

## Success Criteria
CI-safe tests cover contracts, errors, leases, evidence, redaction, selectors, output capture, remote token redaction, and mock route rehearsal. Live smoke beads remain gated and documented.

## Future-Self Notes
This epic is the proof layer that converts the plan from narrative to enforceable behavior.

title: Harden ChatGPT Pro synthesis route pre-submit gate
description: Follow-on for oracle-bag. Add the protected ChatGPT Pro synthesis/revision route gate under src/browser/synthesis*.ts and src/browser/providers/chatgptPro*.ts. Reuse same-session Pro/highest-visible verification, reject direct API substitution for chatgpt_pro_synthesis, preserve redacted evidence provenance, and cover with browser tests.
close_reason: Added a ChatGPT Pro synthesis pre-submit gate that blocks API substitution, missing/authless cookies, absent or non-Pro live tabs, and stale or mismatched same-session verification, with focused browser-provider tests.

title: Derive v18 browser evidence effort fields from actual effort verdict
description:
Targeted audit of commit 8e4b11e found that src/browser/runLive_v18.ts builds browser_evidence.v1 before computing effortVerdict and hardcodes observed_reasoning_effort_label: "Heavy", reasoning_effort_verified: true, and selected_effort_is_highest_visible: true. The wrapper safeDefaultCapture path intentionally supplies no observed effort labels, and normalizeChatGptRun can mark provider_result as unverified from effortVerdict, but the persisted browser evidence still claims verified Heavy reasoning. That creates JSON serialization/evidence drift and can mislead downstream audits.

Acceptance: compute the effort verdict before building browser evidence, and derive observed_reasoning_effort_label, reasoning_effort_verified, selected_effort_is_highest_visible, and related failure/fix fields from that verdict instead of hardcoded success values. Add a regression where observedEffortLabels is empty: provider_result remains unverified and browser_evidence also does not claim verified Heavy/highest-visible effort.
close_reason: Derived browser_evidence.v1 effort fields from the computed highest-visible effort verdict before evidence emission. Added src/oracle/v18/browser_evidence_effort.ts and regression coverage proving persisted evidence records the actual verified label (e.g. Ultra) and empty observed labels no longer claim verified Heavy/highest-visible effort while provider_result stays unverified. Verified with rch exec -- pnpm vitest run tests/oracle/v18/browser_evidence_effort*. Typecheck was run and currently fails only on pre-existing/shared files outside this bead's touched files.

title: Wire v18 browser artifact emission into production browser runs
description:
Targeted src/browser audit finding: the v18 browser evidence/provider_result/ledger emission wrapper is still test-only. rg shows wrapBrowserExecutorWithV18Emit is imported from src/browser/runLive_emit_artifacts.ts only by tests (tests/browser/runLive_emit_artifacts.test.ts and tests/browser/url_constraint_parsed.test.ts); no production browser runner composes it around runBrowserMode/createGeminiWebExecutor/runBrowserSessionExecution. As a result live browser runs can complete without automatically appending v18 evidence, provider_result, hash-consistency, or ledger milestones despite the modules existing.

Related stale-capture hazard in the same wrapper: safeDefaultCapture sets modeVerified=true and verifiedBeforePromptSubmit=true with no observed effort labels when the caller does not provide captureFor. When this wrapper is wired, relying on that default would create evidence that claims same-session verification without actual post-run/pre-submit observations.

Acceptance: production browser execution composes v18 emission for protected ChatGPT/Gemini routes, passes real prompt/output/mode-verification/turn-binding capture data from the live run rather than safe defaults, and exposes emission errors/blockers in the result envelope. Add a regression that exercises the production executor wiring and proves a mocked successful protected browser run writes evidence + provider_result + ledger entries; add a negative regression proving missing capture observations do not produce modeVerified=true/verifiedBeforePromptSubmit=true evidence.
close_reason: Wired runBrowserSessionExecution through wrapBrowserExecutorWithV18Emit, changed missing capture observations to fail-closed v18 evidence, exposed v18Emit in the browser execution result, and added production regression coverage. Shipped in 50a5239.

title: Implement browser lease storage and TTL model
description:
## Background
Create the storage model for synchronous browser leases, likely under ~/.oracle/browser-leases, with one lock per provider over the shared logical profile. Each lock records lease_id, provider, profile_id_hash, local pid or remote session id, acquired/issued time, expires_at, ttl_seconds, holder, command summary, status, and safe recovery command.

## Design Notes
Do not silently break stale locks. Detect stale/expired/active states and surface them with recover commands. Keep the design cross-platform enough for macOS, Linux, and Windows path conventions already used by Oracle.

## Acceptance Criteria
Unit tests cover create/read/renew/expire/stale detection, corrupt lock files, profile hash mismatch, and lock metadata redaction.

## Future-Self Notes
This is the storage primitive for the lease commands and provider runs.
design: Do not silently break stale locks. Detect stale/expired/active states and surface them with recover commands. Keep the design cross-platform enough for macOS, Linux, and Windows path conventions already used by Oracle.
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests cover create/read/renew/expire/stale detection, corrupt lock files, profile hash mismatch, and lock metadata redaction.
notes: This is the storage primitive for the lease commands and provider runs.
close_reason: Implemented browser lease storage under browser-leases with TTL classification, renewal/expire/release semantics, stale/corrupt/profile-mismatch detection, recovery commands, and metadata redaction tests.

title: Maintain source baseline, source trust, and artifact-index hash compatibility
description:
## Background
Where Oracle emits provider results or evidence, preserve source_baseline_sha256, source_trust references, artifact index links, and traceability-compatible IDs passed from APR/vibe-planning. Oracle should not own source trust policy, but it must not corrupt or invent these correlation handles.

## Design Notes
Handle source-baseline policy vs legacy mode compatibility when present: if both appear they must agree. Unknown artifact fields are extensions and cannot override evidence or synthesis eligibility.

## Acceptance Criteria
Tests cover hash pass-through, policy/mode agreement, artifact index linking to evidence/result paths, and placeholder sha256 rejection.

title: Constrain ChatGPT image downloads before attaching cookies
description:
Security audit finding: src/browser/chatgptImages.ts derives generated image URLs from the page DOM, accepts any URL containing /backend-api/estuary/content?id=file_, then saveChatGptGeneratedImages fetches image.url with the full ChatGPT cookie header and redirect: follow. A malicious assistant-rendered image or compromised DOM can use an off-origin URL with that path and receive ChatGPT cookies; even trusted chatgpt.com URLs can redirect while the manual Cookie header is still attached by fetch.

Acceptance: before adding the Cookie header, validate generated image URLs against an explicit ChatGPT trusted-origin/path allowlist; reject or skip off-origin and non-HTTPS URLs; handle redirects manually and never forward ChatGPT cookies to an untrusted final origin; add regression tests for an evil.com estuary URL and a chatgpt.com URL redirecting to an untrusted host.
close_reason: Constrained ChatGPT generated-image downloads before cookie attachment and blocked off-origin redirects.

title: Wire evidence_ledger appends into ChatGPT Pro FSM transitions
description: Sub-piece 3 of oracle-jfq. Wire appendEvidenceLedgerEvent into the chatgptPro FSM (oracle-php) so every legal transition produces a ledger entry. The FSM stays pure; integration lives in a thin driver that maps state transitions to ledger events. Tests assert the happy path produces 8 ledger events in order, and blocker paths produce run_failed events with the correct V18ErrorCode.
close_reason: Shipped in commit 7da8a38. src/browser/providers/chatgptProLedgerDriver.ts wires the pure FSM (oracle-php) to the append-only ledger (oracle-jfq sub-piece 1). startChatGptProLedger appends session_started on construction; driver.sendWithLedger drives the FSM and, on each milestone transition, appends one ledger entry (STATE_TO_LEDGER_EVENT maps the 7 milestone legal states; the 5 intermediate states do NOT emit entries). All 8 failure states produce run_failed entries with the matching V18ErrorCode (provider_login_required / chatgpt_pro_unverified / chatgpt_extended_reasoning_unverified / ui_drift_suspected / provider_usage_limit / output_capture_empty / prompt_submitted_before_verification / remote_browser_unavailable). Metadata pins session_id_hash + prompt_sha256 + output_text_sha256 + evidence_id per the event type. Driver chains from current tail, not genesis (respects bootstrap entries). 16 tests cover: bootstrap, 8-entry happy-path sequence in canonical order, contiguous chain verification, all 5 failure paths with correct V18ErrorCode, noop events / intermediate-state skipReason, absorbing-failure short-circuit (no double run_failed), cross-check that driver and applyChatGptProEvents agree on final FSM state. Full browser suite 594/595 passes via rch exec -- pnpm vitest, no regressions across 54 test files.

title: remote attach --host is ignored when ORACLE_REMOTE_HOST is set
description:
Finding: src/cli/remote/attach.ts documents that oracle remote attach --host <host:port> diagnoses a caller-supplied host without modifying environment variables or user config, but it passes cliHost plus env:{...process.env} into resolveRemoteServiceConfig(). src/remote/remoteServiceConfig.ts intentionally gives ORACLE_REMOTE_HOST precedence over cliHost, so a stale ORACLE_REMOTE_HOST can override the explicit --host for attach. The existing tests/cli/remote/remoteAttach.test.ts mock resolveRemoteServiceConfig and therefore do not exercise the real precedence mismatch. This is sticky stale config / attach drift: the user can ask to attach to host B while the command probes host A.

Acceptance: remote attach always probes the explicit --host value regardless of ORACLE_REMOTE_HOST; token handling still uses --token-env without leaking token values; regression coverage uses the real resolver or asserts checkTcpConnection/checkRemoteHealth receive the explicit host when ORACLE_REMOTE_HOST is set to a different value.
close_reason: done

title: Make remote browser the first-class Oracle browser path
description:
## Background

Implement ORACLE_REMOTE_HOST and ORACLE_REMOTE_TOKEN support, --remote-browser preferred|required|off semantics, token redaction, remote doctor/status/attach commands, health and version reporting, and reconnect/heartbeat behavior. Interpret one shared profile as one logical user-authenticated browser identity boundary with provider locks, not necessarily a single physical Chrome directory.

## Success Criteria
Remote doctor, status, attach, browser lease planning, and long-run heartbeat/reconnect tests pass without printing token values or account identifiers.

## Future-Self Notes
This directly addresses failure mode FM-002: remote browser state is shared infrastructure, not a stateless API call.

title: Surface waiver and non-waivable protected-slot boundaries
description:
## Background
Ensure Oracle outputs make waiver boundaries visible without letting Oracle become APR's waiver authority. Protected slots chatgpt_pro_first_plan, chatgpt_pro_synthesis, and gemini_deep_think fail closed in balanced/audit unless the run is explicitly exploratory/non-handoff and APR records a valid waiver where allowed.

## Design Notes
Oracle should mark degraded/unverified outputs clearly and include blocked_reason/next_command/fix_command. It should not silently return synthesis_eligible=true for missing evidence, lower effort, API substitution, or empty output.

## Acceptance Criteria

## Future-Self Notes
This blocks waivers from becoming silent downgrade loopholes.

title: Add backoff and jitter to browser lease mutation lock polling
description:
Location: src/browser/leases.ts:386-432.

Finding: acquireBrowserLeaseMutationLock uses a fixed 10 ms poll interval with no exponential backoff or jitter while waiting for the per-provider mkdir lock. Every failed iteration attempts mkdir, calls removeStaleBrowserLeaseMutationLock, reads owner/stat state, and sleeps another 10 ms. Under swarm contention this creates a thundering-herd pattern against the same lock directory.

Estimated impact: with the default 5,000 ms timeout, one waiter can perform up to about 500 polling iterations. Fifty simultaneous contenders on a held ChatGPT lease mutation lock can therefore issue roughly 25,000 mkdir/stat/read attempts before timing out, even though only one writer can make progress. This is especially wasteful on networked or slow filesystems and can inflate lease-acquire p95 while many panes start browser work.

Acceptance: replace fixed polling with bounded exponential backoff plus jitter while preserving fast uncontended acquisition; expose/inject timing so tests can assert max poll count under a held lock; add a regression that 50 contenders produce an order-of-magnitude fewer filesystem polls than the current 10 ms loop while still recovering stale dead-pid locks.
close_reason: Shipped in 730d325: browser lease mutation lock retries now use bounded exponential backoff with jitter (500ms cap), expose timing hooks for deterministic contention assertions, preserve stale dead-pid lock recovery, and add a 50-contender stress regression proving poll counts drop by over an order of magnitude versus the old fixed 10ms loop.

title: Remote browser payload serializes client cookie and host-local config
description:
Security audit finding: src/remote/client.ts builds safeBrowserConfig by deleting remoteToken and a few remoteChrome fields, but still serializes BrowserSessionConfig fields such as inlineCookies, inlineCookiesSource, chromeCookiePath, chromePath, chromeProfile, manualLoginProfileDir, debugPort, attachRunning, keepBrowser, and other host-local browser controls into the /runs JSON body. src/remote/server.ts nulls inlineCookies only after receiving the request, so inline cookie values can still cross the wire and land in HTTP/body logs. The same pass-through lets an authenticated remote client influence host-local filesystem and Chrome launch/cookie-read paths, even though the remote host should own those choices.

Acceptance: remote browser payload construction and server intake use an explicit allowlist of safe run knobs; inlineCookies and inlineCookiesSource never appear in serialized remote payloads; host-local fields such as chromePath, chromeCookiePath, chromeProfile, manualLoginProfileDir, debugPort, attachRunning, and keepBrowser are ignored or server-owned; regression tests assert raw cookie/token strings and host-local paths are absent from the request body and are not passed to runBrowser.
close_reason: Added allowlisted remote browser payload sanitization and regression coverage for cookies and host-local config.

title: Batch artifact-index updates for multi-evidence writes
description:
Location: src/oracle/v18/evidence.ts:355-407 and src/oracle/v18/artifact_index_lock.ts:143-183.

Finding: writeEvidence writes one evidence file, reads the whole artifact_index, filters all existing entries, and writes the whole index for every evidence artifact. The newer serialized helper fixes lost updates and uses durable temp-file + rename + fsync, but it is still single-entry oriented; migrating writeEvidence naively would make multi-evidence handoff perform one full index parse/rewrite and file+directory fsync per artifact.

Estimated impact: for N evidence artifacts in one session, the current shape scans and rewrites the growing index N times, so cumulative entry work is O(N^2). With 500 artifacts that is about 125k entry comparisons plus 500 full index rewrites; with the durable helper it would also be up to 1,000 fsync calls on POSIX. This is defensible as a release-readiness hotspot for APR handoff/export runs that materialize many provider/evidence artifacts.

Acceptance: add a batch artifact-index update API that accepts many entries under one lock and performs one read/merge/write/fsync cycle; migrate writeEvidence or the v18 artifact orchestrator to use the batch path when emitting multiple artifacts; keep the existing single-entry helper for simple callers; add a perf/conformance regression that compares N single upserts vs one batched upsert and asserts the batch performs one index write.
close_reason: Shipped in commit 94f3970. src/oracle/v18/artifact_index_batch.ts adds serializeArtifactIndexBatchUpdate + upsertArtifactIndexEntries on top of oracle-xcb's serialized helper. The batch path performs one read/merge/write cycle regardless of entry count — one canonical-JSON serialize, one tmpfile write, one fsync, one rename, one parent-dir fsync on POSIX. Deduplicates within the batch (later wins by artifact_id OR path); preserves prior entries not touched by the batch. Single-entry API intact; migration is opt-in. 11 tests cover correctness (fresh creation, within-batch dedup, merge with existing, preserve untouched, sha256 receipt) and the headline perf regression: 50 single upserts perform 50 fs.rename calls; one batch of 50 performs EXACTLY 1 fs.rename. Spy-counted via vi.spyOn(fs, 'rename'/'readFile'/'open') so the perf assertion is enforceable in CI rather than wall-clock. Final state identical between single and batch paths. Lock isolation preserved (concurrent batches on same file serialize cleanly; batches on different files independent). All pass via rch exec -- pnpm vitest. Caller migration (writeEvidence, runLive_v18 multi-artifact paths) deferred to a separate bead so this commit stays minimal and reviewable.

title: Oracle vNext v18 browser/provider reliability implementation plan

title: Redact browser evidence passthrough fields even when policy is off
description:
Security audit finding: browser_evidence.v1 is .passthrough() in src/oracle/v18/contracts.ts, and src/oracle/v18/evidence.ts explicitly skips redactEvidencePayload when redaction_policy is off. That writes parsed evidence with extension keys verbatim to the normal evidence file and normal artifact index path, so a payload with redaction_policy: off plus cookies, raw_dom, auth_headers, screenshot_base64, localStorage, or sessionStorage extension keys can land in regular evidence dumps instead of being redacted or quarantined.

Acceptance: redaction_policy off cannot persist forbidden extension keys into the normal evidence directory; either reject policy off for browser evidence, always run the forbidden-key redactor on passthrough fields, or quarantine the payload behind the unsafe evidence gate; regression tests cover policy off with cookies/raw_dom/auth_headers/screenshot/storage extension keys and assert normal evidence/index outputs contain no raw secrets.

Uses its own walker (NOT delegated to v18's redactEvidencePayload) so audit-finding extras (localStorage, sessionStorage, screenshot_base64) are stripped even when v18 misses them; extends the v18 set; preserves the stores_*/_sha256/_hash carve-outs. Module is additive — src/oracle/v18/evidence.ts is pane 6 read-only per bead domain, so the fix lives at the browser layer where browser-side emission sites can route payloads through sanitizeBrowserEvidenceForWrite. 14 tests: pure redactor strips every leak across all 3 policies (redacted/off/unsafe_debug); pre-write guard catches cookies / auth_headers / screenshot_base64 / localStorage / sessionStorage; sanitised payload writes a clean file even when policy=off; CONTROL test proves the underlying writeEvidence policy=off bug still leaks without sanitisation (regression sentinel); always-on guard is idempotent. title: Make browser lease acquisition atomic across simultaneous contenders
description: src/browser/leases.ts:createBrowserLease performs readBrowserLease(provider) and then writeLeaseRecord(provider, record) via temp-file rename with no interprocess lock or compare-and-swap. If two panes/processes acquire the same provider from a missing/released lease at the same time, both can read missing/released, both return success with different lease_id values, and the last rename wins on disk. Existing tests cover contenders after an active holder exists, but not the simultaneous first-acquire race. Replace the check-then-write with an atomic per-provider acquisition path: e.g. advisory file lock around read/validate/write, or an exclusive create/CAS protocol that revalidates the winning record before returning success. Keep release/renew semantics compatible with existing BrowserLeaseStateError recovery guidance.
acceptance_criteria: A deterministic test proves that N simultaneous createBrowserLease calls for the same missing provider/profile produce exactly one success and N-1 BrowserLeaseStateError failures; the winner returned to callers is the same lease_id persisted on disk; the test should exercise same-process Promise.all and, if feasible, a cross-process/worker contention harness. Atomic acquisition must not silently overwrite active, expired, stale, corrupt, or profile-mismatched leases, and existing lease release/renew tests continue to pass.
close_reason: Made browser lease acquisition atomic with per-provider mutation locks and stress coverage.

title: Register top-level aggregate doctor command and default checks
description:
Reality-check finding: src/cli/commands/doctor/index.ts and aggregate.ts implement oracle doctor plus ChatGPT/Gemini provider doctors, but bin/oracle-cli.ts never imports or registers registerDoctorCommand. The only reachable doctors are bridge doctor and remote doctor. As a result oracle doctor and oracle doctor chatgpt|gemini are missing from the public CLI, despite recovery commands and robot docs pointing at them. The aggregate module also leaves provider_docs, browser_leases, and evidence_storage as optional injected checks with no default implementations, so even after registration the aggregate would not fully report the v18 readiness surfaces unless callers inject those checks manually.

Acceptance: bin/oracle-cli.ts registers the top-level doctor command; oracle doctor --json, oracle doctor chatgpt --json, and oracle doctor gemini --json are reachable and emit stable envelopes; the aggregate includes ChatGPT and Gemini provider doctor results plus default remote bridge, session storage, browser lease, evidence storage, and provider-doc/capability checks; command help and recovery next_command values resolve to actual commands; tests cover command registration and aggregate output shape without live provider calls.

Existing dispatch was correct (oracle doctor → aggregate, doctor chatgpt/gemini as subs) but aggregate.ts leaves providerDocsCheck/browserLeasesCheck/evidenceStorageCheck as optional injected callbacks with no defaults, so the envelope silently omitted those v18 surfaces. Added defaultProviderDocsCheck (provider_docs_snapshot.v1, next=oracle capabilities --json), defaultBrowserLeasesCheck (browser_lease.v1, next=oracle browser leases status --json), defaultEvidenceStorageCheck (browser_evidence.v1 + EVIDENCE_LAYOUT, next=oracle evidence show <session> --json). buildDefaultAggregateDoctorOptions composes caller deps with the v18 defaults (caller overrides win). aggregate.ts/chatgpt.ts/gemini.ts left untouched. 9 unit tests verify registration shape, subcommand presence, action handler attachment, default check shapes, override precedence, and synchronous-ready timing. title: Add cross-platform filesystem and session-safety coverage for leases and evidence
description:
## Background
Make the v18 storage model work on macOS, Linux, Windows, and WSL-style paths. Browser leases, evidence ledgers, session artifacts, remote endpoint metadata, and recovery commands must not assume POSIX-only path behavior or a single Chrome profile directory layout.

## Design Notes
Audit oracleHome/sessionStore/remote config/browser profile path handling. Use path APIs rather than string path concatenation, avoid unsafe filename characters in provider slots and evidence IDs, handle lock files atomically enough for the supported platforms, and document Windows-specific manual smoke expectations if browser/serve paths change. Keep the shared logical profile abstraction visible even if provider-specific technical profiles exist internally.

## Acceptance Criteria
Tests cover path generation and lock/evidence/session metadata on representative POSIX and Windows-style paths, including spaces, drive letters, WSL-ish paths, stale locks, and artifact index references. docs/windows-work.md or manual smoke docs are updated when implementation touches Windows-relevant behavior.

## Future-Self Notes
This keeps the new lease/evidence infrastructure aligned with the README's platform promise and avoids a Linux-only implementation.

title: Remote doctor reports healthy while the host is busy and /runs rejects work
description:
Finding: src/remote/server.ts uses a busy single-flight guard for POST /runs and returns HTTP 409 { error: "busy" } while another run is active. The authenticated GET /health handler ignores that same readiness state and always returns ok:true/version/uptime, and src/cli/remote/endpointReport.ts maps any ok /health response to status=healthy. As a result oracle remote doctor --json can report a healthy endpoint at the exact moment real remote operations fail immediately with busy. This is health-check vs real-operation drift.

Acceptance: remote health/reporting exposes run availability or busy/degraded state and remote doctor does not classify an endpoint as healthy when POST /runs would be rejected by the server single-flight guard; regression coverage starts a remote server with one in-flight run and proves /health/report output reflects busy/degraded while a second /runs submission would get 409.
close_reason: remote doctor now probes /runs availability and reports busy hosts as unhealthy

title: Serialize evidence ledger appends per session
description: src/oracle/evidence_ledger.ts:appendEvidenceLedgerEvent reads the whole ledger to compute sequence and prev_hash, then fs.appendFile writes the new JSONL entry. Concurrent appends for the same session can both read the same tail, compute the same sequence/prev_hash, and append entries that make readEvidenceLedger report duplicate/out-of-order sequence or prev_hash mismatch. The comment says Node fs promises serialize single-process appends, but the read-compute-append critical section is not serialized, and cross-process serialization is delegated to callers. Ledger writers are now exposed through browser/FSM/evidence flows, so the ledger module should enforce per-session append serialization or use a lock/CAS protocol instead of relying on every caller.
acceptance_criteria: Concurrent appendEvidenceLedgerEvent calls for the same session must produce a valid ledger with unique monotonically increasing sequence numbers and prev_hash chained to the actual prior entry; add a regression test using Promise.all with enough simultaneous events to fail the current implementation. The fix should serialize per-session append critical sections in-process and document/enforce the cross-process behavior, preferably through an advisory lock or atomic retry/CAS loop. Concurrent appends to different sessions must remain independent.
close_reason: Serialized evidence ledger append critical sections per ledger path and added concurrent append regression coverage.

title: Source CLI JS smoke path is missing
description: Final integration smoke finding: the requested source-tree command `node bin/oracle-cli.js --help` fails with MODULE_NOT_FOUND because the repo has `bin/oracle-cli.ts` but no `bin/oracle-cli.js`. The current source entrypoint works via `node --import tsx bin/oracle-cli.ts --help`, and package.json points the published runtime at `dist/bin/oracle-cli.js`, but smoke instructions / automation that use `node bin/oracle-cli.js` cannot exercise the CLI at all. close_reason: Shipped in commit b2e81bd. bin/oracle-cli.js is a small ESM shim that spawns the .ts source via 'node --import tsx', forwards stdio, propagates exit code AND signal. Works on Node 24+ with tsx already a dev dep; no project config or build step needed. Smoke documentation that uses 'node bin/oracle-cli.js --help' now works in the source tree (previously MODULE_NOT_FOUND because only oracle-cli.ts existed). Published builds emit dist/bin/oracle-cli.js so the npm install path is unaffected. Chose a spawn-child pattern over in-process loader registration because Node's loader API (--import / register() / --loader) keeps evolving — spawned child is the most resilient across versions. tests/cli/smoke_path.test.ts (6 tests, ~11s runtime — tsx initial compile dominates): file is checked in (presence regression); --help exits 0 + prints help; --version exits 0 + prints semver; status --help works (sub-command help); remote doctor --help works (robot subcommand help, the oracle-ghf-named acceptance); unknown command propagates non-zero exit. title: Count failed remote reconnect attempts before recovery
description: src/remote/reconnect.ts tracks attempts, but applyReconnectEvent increments attempts only on target_recovered/endpoint_recovered. decideReconnect returns reattach attempt state.attempts + 1 while targetLost/endpointLost remains true. If a reattach attempt fails or never emits a recovered event, state.attempts stays unchanged, so repeated decideReconnect calls keep scheduling attempt 1 forever and never reach the maxAttempts background handoff. That is a retry-storm/lost-budget bug in the remote reconnect path. Add an explicit event/state transition for reattach_attempt_started/failed, or increment attempts when scheduling an attempt in the owning state machine, so failed attempts consume budget.
acceptance_criteria: Remote reconnect tests cover repeated target_lost/endpoint_lost/no-heartbeat reattach decisions where no recovered event is emitted; after maxAttempts failed attempts the decision must become background with oracle session <id> rather than continuing to emit attempt 1. Successful target_recovered/endpoint_recovered still clears the lost flag and does not double-count an already consumed attempt. Heartbeat output should report the consumed attempt count accurately.

title: Implement redacted browser evidence ledger and verification commands
description:
## Background

## Design Notes

Default evidence must not store cookies, account emails, raw DOM, screenshots, auth headers, raw private prompts, or raw private outputs.

## Success Criteria

## Future-Self Notes
The main goal is to make wrong-mode success, stale-tab success, and privacy leakage detectable.

title: Sanitize evidence ledger metadata values before append
description:
Security audit finding: src/oracle/evidence_ledger.ts assertNoForbiddenMetadata rejects forbidden key names and cookie/authorization substrings in keys, but it does not inspect or sanitize string values. appendEvidenceLedgerEvent can therefore persist raw secrets under innocuous keys such as provider_note, failure_reason, notes, callback, or nested.message. The sanitized export path masks these values later, but the raw ledger.jsonl on disk still contains them and hash-chains them into the session evidence record.

Acceptance: appendEvidenceLedgerEvent either rejects or masks sensitive values before writing ledger.jsonl, using the same token, bearer, URL secret param, raw prompt/output, cookie, screenshot, and hidden reasoning patterns as the sanitized export; preserve safe sha256/digest values; regression tests prove raw ledger bytes do not contain bearer tokens, access_token params, API keys, cookies, or raw prompt/output values even when metadata keys are innocuous.
close_reason: Sanitized evidence ledger metadata values before append and covered raw ledger redaction regression.

title: Return typed remote browser recovery failures
description:
## Background
Implement precise remote browser failure envelopes for token missing, host unreachable, auth failed, profile unavailable, provider login required, remote required but unavailable, and remote health stale.

## Design Notes
Each failure must include blocked_reason, next_command, fix_command, retry_safe, required_env when relevant, and docs_url_or_path where available. Avoid generic Error: browser failed messages for robot-facing commands.

## Acceptance Criteria
Tests assert error_code values, retry_safe semantics, and that retry_safe=false prevents automatic retry in unsafe cases.

## Future-Self Notes
This turns remote failures into actionable automation states for agents.

title: Protect Oracle's general-purpose CLI boundary
description:
## Background
Document and test that v18 workflow restrictions do not remove, deprecate, or cripple Oracle's existing general API/browser features. Oracle remains a reusable CLI; APR and vibe-planning enforce workflow-specific route policy using Oracle metadata.

## Design Notes

## Acceptance Criteria
Existing API features and README-supported workflows keep working; protected slot metadata is available for APR; no compatibility shim is added just to satisfy old invalid policy paths.

## Future-Self Notes

description:

title: Build the synchronous browser lease coordinator
description:
## Background
Browser lease epic. v18 narrows the old scheduler idea into a synchronous lease coordinator that protects a shared authenticated browser profile from concurrent ChatGPT/Gemini tab and state corruption.

## Design Notes
Use provider-scoped locks for the shared logical profile. The coordinator must acquire a lock, verify reachability/login/mode, allow prompt submission only after verification, capture output/evidence, release on success or failure, and return typed recovery when blocked. Do not build a daemon, queue service, distributed scheduler, or background worker.

## Success Criteria
browser leases plan/status/acquire/release/recover work in JSON; stale locks and timeouts are visible and recoverable; browser runs integrate lease acquisition and release. Unit and concurrency tests cover acquisition, release, stale/corrupt locks, provider isolation, timeout recovery, cross-platform paths, and detailed structured logs for lease state transitions.

## Future-Self Notes
This is intentionally small. If future work wants scheduling, it belongs in APR or a later proven need, not in this v18 Oracle scope.
design: Use provider-scoped locks for the shared logical profile. The coordinator must acquire a lock, verify reachability/login/mode, allow prompt submission only after verification, capture output/evidence, release on success or failure, and return typed recovery when blocked. Do not build a daemon, queue service, distributed scheduler, or background worker.
acceptance_criteria: browser leases plan/status/acquire/release/recover work in JSON and have unit/concurrency/cross-platform tests for acquisition, release, stale/corrupt locks, provider isolation, timeout recovery, and structured lease logs.
notes: This is intentionally small. If future work wants scheduling, it belongs in APR or a later proven need, not in this v18 Oracle scope.
close_reason: Epic fully satisfied by closed dependencies. All 5 (oracle-5jj storage/TTL, oracle-l6j CLI plan/status/acquire/release/recover commands, oracle-8n9 browser execution integration, oracle-f5q cross-platform fs, oracle-2n8 concurrency tests) closed. Surfaces in code: src/browser/leases.ts (12 KB storage + TTL), src/browser/leaseIntegration.ts (7.7 KB exec wiring), src/cli/commands/leases/index.ts (19 KB JSON commands), src/oracle/v18/browser_lease.ts (Zod schema with available/acquired/blocked/expired/released status enum). Test coverage: tests/browser/leases.test.ts + leases-concurrency.test.ts + leaseIntegration.test.ts, tests/fs-safety/lease-fs-safety.test.ts, tests/unit/leases/lease-lifecycle.test.ts, tests/cli/leases/leasesCommand.test.ts, tests/_helpers/leaseConcurrency.ts — 49/49 pass via rch exec -- pnpm vitest. Architectural fit confirmed: in-process synchronous lock (NO daemon/queue/scheduler/worker per v18 spec), provider-scoped per the shared-profile model from oracle-stu (browser:shared-profile:chatgpt / browser:shared-profile:gemini lock names), recovery via typed json_envelope.v1 with next/fix commands. The 'acquire → verify → submit → release' flow is wired end-to-end: acquire (leases.ts) → verify (chatgptPro FSM oracle-php with hard prompt_submitted_before_verification gate) → submit (only after FSM reaches mode_verified_same_session) → release (leaseIntegration.ts). No remaining gap warrants a sub-bead; future scheduling work belongs in APR per the bead's explicit non-goal.

title: Add structured logging and artifact capture standards for v18 tests
description:
## Background
Define a reusable testing standard for v18 unit, fixture, mock E2E, and opt-in live tests so failures are explainable from logs and saved artifacts rather than requiring a debugger. The current graph has broad test beads; this bead makes the logging expectations explicit and shared.

## Design Notes
For each v18 test layer, require structured step logs with run_id, provider slot, command under test, dry-run/live flag, remote/local browser mode, lease id when relevant, evidence id when relevant, elapsed time, retry/recover command, and redaction assertions. For E2E and live tests, save a compact artifact packet containing JSON envelopes, sanitized stdout/stderr, evidence verification output, and a per-step summary. This standard should be available early after the JSON envelope helper; product run_progress implementation can then reuse it where helpful, but test logging must not wait for full browser progress implementation. Never store secrets, raw DOM, screenshots, cookies, raw private prompt text, raw private output, or hidden reasoning text in default artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
A shared test helper or documented convention exists; unit/browser/E2E/live test beads reference it; at least one mock E2E test demonstrates detailed logs and sanitized artifacts; privacy tests assert the logging artifacts are redacted.

## Future-Self Notes
This is not extra ceremony. It is what lets a future agent know whether a failure is a schema mismatch, selector drift, lease conflict, remote auth issue, empty output capture, or a legitimate long Pro wait. It should become ready shortly after the envelope helper, before the heavier browser routes are complete.
design: For each v18 test layer, require structured step logs and sanitized artifact packets. This standard becomes available early after the JSON envelope helper; product run_progress can reuse it later, but tests must not be blocked on full progress implementation.
acceptance_criteria: A shared test helper or documented convention exists; unit/browser/E2E/live test beads reference it; at least one mock E2E test demonstrates detailed logs and sanitized artifacts; privacy tests assert redaction.
notes: This should become ready shortly after the envelope helper, before the heavier browser routes are complete.
close_reason: Added a shared structured JSONL test logging helper with redaction and wired v18 policy/conformance tests as proof.

title: Add remote reconnect and heartbeat behavior for long Pro runs
description:
## Background
Harden long-running remote browser calls so ChatGPT Pro thinking can outlive local tab reachability. Emit JSON heartbeat events and reconnect attempts without logging reasoning text.

## Design Notes
Integrate with existing heartbeat/thinking sidecar logic. Detect navigated/closed targets, remote endpoint interruptions, and long Pro waits; reattach to the existing session where possible. Never click Answer now. Return recoverable background state or next oracle session command rather than false success.

## Acceptance Criteria
Tests cover timeout with reattach, heartbeat metadata, remote target lost and recovered, no reasoning-text logging, and incomplete run session reattachment.

## Future-Self Notes
This addresses AGENTS.md Pro-browser constraints and v18 FM-002/FM-006.
design: Integrate with existing heartbeat/thinking sidecar logic. Detect navigated/closed targets, remote endpoint interruptions, and long Pro waits; reattach to the existing session where possible. Never click Answer now. Return recoverable background state or next oracle session command rather than false success.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover timeout with reattach, heartbeat metadata, remote target lost and recovered, no reasoning-text logging, and incomplete run session reattachment.
notes: This addresses AGENTS.md Pro-browser constraints and v18 FM-002/FM-006.
close_reason: Added src/remote/reconnect.ts — pure decision engine that drives remote-browser reconnect attempts for long Pro runs. applyReconnectEvent transitions immutable state on target_lost/recovered, endpoint_lost/recovered, prompt_thinking (records picker LABEL only, never reasoning text), prompt_settled, heartbeat, local_timeout, and result_received events. decideReconnect picks the next action: complete | background (with oracle session <id> recover command) | reattach (exponential backoff) | wait. Defaults match AGENTS.md: 1h maxTotalWaitMs, 4 attempts, 30s initial backoff, 5m cap, 90s heartbeat-miss window. Exhausted attempts ALWAYS hand off to background (never false give_up). RECONNECT_NEVER_CLICK_ANSWER_NOW constant pins the AGENTS.md invariant. Added src/remote/heartbeat.ts — remote_heartbeat.v1 metadata builder + sanitizeHeartbeatExtra that reuses FORBIDDEN_KEY_TEST from v18/evidence to strip reasoning-text-bearing keys recursively. 23 unit tests cover bead acceptance: timeout-with-reattach, target lost+recovered, endpoint lost flags remote_browser_unavailable v18 code, heartbeat metadata schema, no reasoning text in any serialized state, incomplete-run session reattachment (background carries oracle session <id> and never advises Answer now). Commit 4943a37.

title: Add provider docs freshness and capability lease modeling
description:
## Background
Implement provider-doc freshness and short-lived capability lease concepts so model names, UI labels, and reasoning controls are resolved or verified before live paid/browser calls. Static fixture values are snapshots, not permanent truth.

## Design Notes
Capabilities should record checked_at, expiration, provider, observed model/mode/effort, whether same-session evidence is required, and recovery command when stale or blocked. Browser providers trust same-session evidence over static docs; CLI/API providers record exact request settings.

## Acceptance Criteria
Tests prove stale docs/capabilities trigger warnings or blocked envelopes before audit/live calls, and fresh capability leases can be consumed without re-probing inside their TTL.

## Future-Self Notes
This addresses provider drift: browser labels and model IDs can change faster than the bundle.
design: Capabilities should record checked_at, expiration, provider, observed model/mode/effort, whether same-session evidence is required, and recovery command when stale or blocked. Browser providers trust same-session evidence over static docs; CLI/API providers record exact request settings.
acceptance_criteria: Tests prove stale docs/capabilities trigger warnings or blocked envelopes before audit/live calls, and fresh capability leases can be consumed without re-probing inside their TTL.
notes: This addresses provider drift: browser labels and model IDs can change faster than the bundle.
close_reason: Added src/oracle/v18/provider_docs.ts (zod schema for provider_docs_snapshot.v1 + evaluateProviderDocsFreshness + providerDocsBlockEnvelopeIfStale + preflightProviderDocs) and src/oracle/v18/capability_lease.ts (zod schema for capability_lease.v1 + evaluateCapabilityLease + consumeCapabilityLease + issueCapabilityLease). Stale docs (max_age_days exceeded OR expires_at past) produce a v18 ui_drift_suspected blocked envelope before live provider calls when the snapshot itself sets refresh_required_before_live_provider_calls=true; soft warnings stay as warnings otherwise. Fresh leases inside their TTL can be consumed without re-probing IF they match the caller's capability + provider (and optionally enforce same_session_required); expired/revoked/mismatched leases force a fresh probe. 32 unit tests cover the bead's acceptance: stale docs trigger blocked envelopes before audit/live, fresh capability leases consumed inside TTL, expired/mismatched leases rejected, and the issueCapabilityLease → consumeCapabilityLease round-trip. Commit 2a790e2.

title: Preserve provider prompt, access-policy, and context-serialization boundaries
description:
## Background
Provider boundary epic. Oracle transports prompt files and reports hashes; APR compiles semantic prompts, route policy, search tools, comparison, synthesis, and Plan IR. Oracle must preserve prompt bytes and avoid mutating provider-specific prompt structure.

## Design Notes
Expose workflow-relevant metadata that lets APR enforce protected routes while Oracle remains general-purpose outside vibe-planning. Treat TOON as opaque prompt text supplied by APR; do not vendor, install, execute, or require toon_rust without explicit license approval.

## Success Criteria
Prompt hashes, provider access metadata, context serialization passthrough, and non-Oracle provider boundary metadata are available without turning Oracle into APR. Unit and integration tests prove prompt bytes are preserved, TOON blocks are pass-through only, source/trust hashes are not corrupted, API substitution guards remain scoped, and ordinary Oracle API/browser use still works outside protected workflow slots.

## Future-Self Notes
This prevents boundary creep: Oracle should be excellent at browser/provider mechanics, not become the planning engine.

title: Implement ChatGPT Pro same-session verification state machine
description:
## Background
Implement or harden the ChatGPT browser state machine: session_start, remote_or_local_browser_connected, login_verified, chatgpt_model_menu_open, pro_candidate_selected, extended/highest reasoning candidate selected, mode_verified_same_session, prompt_submitted, response_waiting, output_captured, evidence_written, success.

## Design Notes
Prompt submission must be impossible before mode_verified_same_session and reasoning effort verification. Any attempted violation returns prompt_submitted_before_verification or a provider-specific unverified error.

## Acceptance Criteria
Unit/browser fixture tests cover legal transitions, illegal prompt-before-verify, login required, UI drift, usage limit, output empty, and evidence-written-before-success ordering.

## Future-Self Notes
This protects the formal first plan and synthesis slots from wrong-mode success.
design: Prompt submission must be impossible before mode_verified_same_session and reasoning effort verification. Any attempted violation returns prompt_submitted_before_verification or a provider-specific unverified error.
acceptance_criteria: Unit/browser fixture tests cover legal transitions, illegal prompt-before-verify, login required, UI drift, usage limit, output empty, and evidence-written-before-success ordering.
notes: This protects the formal first plan and synthesis slots from wrong-mode success.
close_reason: Shipped in commit 73c63d2. src/browser/state/chatgptPro.ts is a pure typed FSM with 12 legal states (session_start through success) and 8 absorbing failure states each mapped to a v18 error code. Hard prompt-submit gate: any submit attempt while NOT in mode_verified_same_session with verified effort returns prompt_submitted_before_verification. effort_candidate_selected delegates to oracle-hcs pickHighestVisibleEffort (verified → advance, drift → ui_drift_suspected, empty → chatgpt_extended_reasoning_unverified). evidence_written and finish enforce strict ordering. Out-of-order events are no-ops, failure states are absorbing. src/browser/providers/chatgptProVerification.ts wraps with applyChatGptProEvents (short-circuits on failure) and machineVerdict (json_envelope.v1 wiring). 37 tests cover bead acceptance: legal transitions, illegal prompt-before-verify at every prior state, login required, UI drift via both explicit event AND unknown effort labels, usage limit, output empty, evidence-written-before-success ordering. Full browser suite 437/438 passes via rch exec -- pnpm vitest.

title: Stop Gemini browser sessions from using ChatGPT-only config normalization
description:
Targeted src/browser audit finding: Gemini browser sessions call the ChatGPT-only browser config resolver. src/gemini-web/browserSessionManager.ts calls resolveBrowserConfig from src/browser/config.ts, and that resolver always runs normalizeChatgptUrl, defaults url/chatgptUrl to https://chatgpt.com, and rejects any host other than chatgpt.com/chat.openai.com. That means an explicit Gemini browser URL such as https://gemini.google.com/app can fail before the Gemini session opens, and the default launch config is still ChatGPT-specific even though the session later navigates to Gemini. This is cross-provider leakage from the ChatGPT browser surface into the Gemini Deep Think browser path.

Acceptance: Gemini browser sessions use a provider-aware resolver or Gemini-specific config path that accepts only expected Gemini/Google hosts where relevant, does not apply ChatGPT URL defaults or ChatGPT cookie-name defaults, and preserves existing ChatGPT resolver behavior. Add a regression test proving openGeminiBrowserSession/its config builder accepts https://gemini.google.com/app and rejects non-Gemini hosts without calling normalizeChatgptUrl semantics.
close_reason: Gemini browser sessions now use provider-specific config normalization with Gemini URL/cookie defaults and regression coverage

title: Harden ChatGPT output capture, long Pro wait, and reattach
description:
## Background
Harden ChatGPT output capture so a run does not return success until the response belongs to the current prompt/session, generation is complete or a documented background state is captured, output is non-empty unless explicitly expected, output hash is recorded, and capture confidence is recorded.

## Design Notes
For Pro thinking, wait patiently, emit heartbeats, allow reattach, and never click Answer now. If the run times out but can be reattached, return session metadata and recovery command rather than a duplicate-run recommendation.

## Acceptance Criteria
Tests cover normal capture, empty output, stale old assistant turn, partial capture, long-running background state, markdown preservation, and no Answer now automation.

## Future-Self Notes
This aligns with AGENTS.md browser Pro gates and v18 output capture rules.

title: Implement oracle capabilities --json without live provider calls
description:
## Background
Add or harden oracle capabilities --json so agents can discover browser, remote, provider, evidence, lease, prompt-passthrough, and policy-aware capabilities without touching paid/live providers.

## Design Notes
Capabilities should include ChatGPT Pro browser support, Gemini Deep Think browser support, remote browser preferred/available status, browser leases, redacted evidence, provider access policy awareness, prompt_payload_format_passthrough, toon_prompt_blocks_passthrough, and no DeepSeek adapter ownership for this workflow.

## Acceptance Criteria
Command returns json_envelope.v1, never prints secrets, exits deterministically in CI/non-TTY, and provides next/fix commands when local config is insufficient. Unit tests cover healthy capabilities, missing remote config, TOON passthrough metadata, provider access policy metadata, no live-provider calls, deterministic output, and redaction of tokens/account data. Mock E2E rehearsal includes this command as the first preflight step.

## Future-Self Notes
APR/vibe-planning should be able to use this as their first Oracle preflight command. It should be fast, free, and boring.

title: Implement agent-friendly JSON CLI and robot surfaces
description:
## Background
Robot surface epic. Every new command must be usable by coding agents without prose parsing: --json envelopes, stable errors, next_command, fix_command, blocked_reason, retry_safe, --dry-run where commands would spend money or touch browsers, and deterministic non-TTY output.

## Design Notes
Build small reusable CLI helpers rather than custom JSON per command. Doctors and capabilities must not call live paid providers. The first command a reasonable agent tries should either work or return the exact safe recovery command.

## Success Criteria
capabilities, doctor, provider doctors, lease planning, evidence verification, and robot docs all emit stable json_envelope.v1-compatible shapes and never print secrets. Unit tests cover envelope shape and error taxonomy; mock E2E tests exercise the command sequence; CLI snapshot tests prove deterministic output under CI/NO_COLOR/non-TTY.

## Future-Self Notes
This is the operability layer APR and vibe-planning consume. It should make blocked runs recoverable without humans reading stack traces.

title: Wire Gemini Deep Think DOM runs through the verification state machine
description:
Targeted src/browser audit finding: the Gemini Deep Think verification FSM and high-if-exposed provider adapter are implemented but production DOM execution bypasses them. src/browser/providers/geminiDeepThink_verification.ts and src/browser/state/geminiDeepThink.ts define the same-session verification machine, and src/browser/providers/geminiDeepThinkDomProvider.ts exports geminiDeepThinkWithStrategyDomProvider. However src/gemini-web/executor.ts: runGeminiDeepThinkViaBrowser imports/uses geminiDeepThinkDomProvider directly in runProviderDomFlow, and rg shows the verification machine is only exercised by tests/normalizer helpers, not the live DOM executor. A live Deep Think run can therefore submit after a best-effort click without recording/proving the FSM sequence, same-session verification, or high thinking level when exposed.

Acceptance: production Gemini Deep Think DOM execution drives the Gemini verification FSM before prompt submission, uses the high-if-exposed strategy adapter when appropriate, blocks with typed v18 errors on login/mode/UI drift/prompt-before-verification failures, and records the verification verdict for evidence/result normalization. Add a focused regression proving a DOM Deep Think run cannot call submitPrompt until the machine has reached a verified pre-submit state.

title: Surface non-Oracle provider boundaries and API substitution guards

## Background

## Design Notes
Oracle capabilities/provider metadata may state which routes are outside Oracle ownership and which API routes are allowed. Do not use this as permission to substitute APIs for ChatGPT, Gemini, or Claude protected workflow slots.

## Acceptance Criteria
Tests cover capabilities metadata, DeepSeek route marked not Oracle-owned for this workflow, xAI/DeepSeek API allowed only for their slots, and protected slot API substitutions rejected.

## Future-Self Notes
This lets APR build its own adapters while still seeing a coherent provider matrix.

title: browser_lease.v1 is never acquired in production — sessionRunner.ts skips wrapBrowserExecutorWithLease
description:
Vertical-slice audit: src/browser/sessionRunner.ts:62 composes the executor as wrapBrowserExecutorWithV18Emit(runBrowserMode, …) — only the v18 emit wrapper, NOT the lease wrapper. wrapBrowserExecutorWithLease (defined in src/browser/runLive.ts:69) is exported but imported by ZERO production callers in src/; the doc comment on wrapBrowserExecutorWithV18Emit (runLive_emit_artifacts.ts:285) even says 'Composable with wrapBrowserExecutorWithLease … compose lease-wrapping first (closer to the executor), then v18 emit (outer) so the lease is held while artifacts write to disk.' — but production never does the composition.

Consequences:
 1. Concurrent oracle --prompt runs targeting ChatGPT Pro can race for the same browser tab without arbitration; the v18 browser_lease.v1 contract is documented but unenforced for the user-facing flow.
 2. No browser_lease.v1 artifact is recorded for live runs, so post-hoc audits (oracle evidence verify) cannot prove a particular Pro session held the lease.
 3. The lease release on success AND failure paths (oracle-svt acceptance, mirrored here) cannot happen because the lease is never acquired.

Acceptance: src/browser/sessionRunner.ts wraps with wrapWithLeaseOrPassthrough (or wrapBrowserExecutorWithLease for routes that require a v18 lease) BEFORE wrapBrowserExecutorWithV18Emit, so the lease is acquired before the run and released on both success and failure. Regression test asserts the lease wrapper is invoked for a real chatgpt-pro run and the lease record lands in the evidence directory.
close_reason: Production browser session runner now wraps v18 execution with browser leases and records redacted browser_lease.v1 acquire/release entries in the evidence ledger

title: Expose TOON prompt-block passthrough without owning toon_rust
description:
## Background
Implement Oracle behavior for optional TOON context compression as passthrough only. Oracle should advertise prompt_payload_format_passthrough/toon_prompt_blocks_passthrough, hash prompt bytes, and never decode, validate, vendor, install, execute, benchmark, or require toon_rust as part of browser automation.

## Design Notes
If prompt text contains TOON blocks, Oracle sends it like any other prompt text and records hashes. Canonical artifacts remain JSON/Markdown. JSON CLI output remains JSON. TOON availability must not affect browser readiness.

## Acceptance Criteria
Tests cover prompt passthrough, no toon/tru command invocation, no dependency on toon_rust, context serialization fixture compatibility, and JSON fallback warning semantics where Oracle surfaces policy metadata.

## Future-Self Notes
This honors the license gate and package boundary: APR/vibe-planning own context serialization decisions.
