title: Remote client bypasses payload sanitizer and can send local cookie/profile fields over wire
description:
Finding: src/remote/payload_sanitize.ts defines an allowlist serializer for remote run payloads, and tests/remote/payload_sanitize.test.ts prove fields like inlineCookies, inlineCookiesSource, chromeCookiePath, chromeProfile, manualLoginProfileDir, debugPort, attachRunning, keepBrowser, and browserTabRef must not cross the wire. But src/remote/client.ts still hand-builds safeBrowserConfig by deleting only remoteToken/remoteChrome/remoteChromeBrowserWSEndpoint/remoteChromeProfileRoot, then sends Buffer.from(JSON.stringify(payload)). That means a caller can leak client cookies or local browser/profile paths to the remote host before the host-side sanitizer ever runs. tests/remote/payload.test.ts also contradicts the sanitizer by expecting chromeProfile to survive.

Acceptance: createRemoteBrowserExecutor uses the allowlist sanitizer/serializer for the actual request body; regression coverage injects inlineCookies, inlineCookiesSource, chromeCookiePath, chromeProfile, manualLoginProfileDir, debugPort, attachRunning, keepBrowser, browserTabRef, and remoteToken and proves the captured request body omits them while preserving safe fields like desiredModel/timeoutMs; host-side sanitizeRemoteRunPayloadForHost behavior remains intact.
close_reason: Remote client now serializes run payloads through the wire sanitizer with regression coverage

title: Define evidence storage, artifact index, and redaction defaults
description:
## Background
Implement where redacted evidence ledgers are written, how they are referenced from sessions/artifact indexes, and how redaction defaults are enforced. Evidence for critical browser slots must be findable but private by default.

## Design Notes
Store JSON evidence with stable IDs and hashes. Normal artifact indexes may reference redacted evidence paths and hashes. Unsafe artifacts, if ever requested, go to quarantine and are excluded from normal APR/handoff indexes.

## Acceptance Criteria
Tests cover evidence path creation, artifact index entries, default redacted mode, and absence of forbidden fields: cookies, account email, raw DOM, screenshots, auth headers, raw private prompt/output.

## Future-Self Notes
This is the privacy-preserving evidence foundation.

title: Map existing Oracle code paths to v18 responsibilities
description:
## Background
Create an implementation map from existing Oracle TypeScript modules to v18 responsibilities: CLI command registration, browser ChatGPT automation, gemini-web automation, remote service/client, session storage, heartbeat/logging, MCP surfaces, live tests, and docs/manual tests. This map is the first ready task because it keeps the rest of the graph grounded in the repo that exists today.

## Design Notes
Start by reading src/oracle.ts, src/cli/options.ts, src/browser/*, src/gemini-web/*, src/remote/*, src/session*.ts, src/heartbeat.ts, src/mcp/*, docs/manual-tests.md, and relevant tests. Identify what can be hardened in place and what truly requires new modules. Avoid replacing the browser stack. Include an implementation-slicing table that names likely file ownership boundaries for contracts/envelopes, remote browser, browser leases, evidence, ChatGPT selectors/state, Gemini selectors/state, provider-result normalization, MCP, tests, and docs. Include suggested Agent Mail reservation globs for each track so parallel implementers can reserve files by bead ID before editing.

## Acceptance Criteria
A short internal implementation note or bead comment identifies target files/modules for each major v18 surface, recommended implementation order, file ownership/reservation guidance, and any areas where current code already satisfies part of v18. No product code changes are required by this bead unless a local convention already has an architecture note location.

## Future-Self Notes
This should prevent duplicate abstractions, reduce merge conflicts, and keep implementation sympathetic to the current repo. It should also remind future agents that root AGENTS.md forbids deleting files and script-based code rewrites, so large mechanical changes need careful manual slicing or disjoint worker ownership.

title: Serialize evidence artifact index updates during concurrent writes
description: src/oracle/v18/evidence.ts:writeEvidence writes the evidence file, then reads index.json, upserts one ArtifactIndexEntry, and writes index.json back. Concurrent writeEvidence calls for different evidence_id values in the same session can both read the same prior index and last-write-wins one entry out of the artifact index, leaving an evidence file on disk that APR/evidence verification cannot discover. writeArtifactIndex also writes the target file directly rather than an atomic temp+rename+directory-fsync sequence, so readers can observe partial/crash-lost index state depending on platform. This is distinct from the evidence ledger JSONL chain race: artifact index updates need their own per-session/index serialization or CAS retry.
acceptance_criteria: A regression test runs concurrent writeEvidence calls for the same session with distinct evidence_id values and proves every evidence file appears in the correct normal or quarantine artifact index. The fix serializes per-index read/modify/write or uses an atomic retry/CAS protocol, writes indexes via temp file + rename, and fsyncs file/directory where supported without breaking Windows. Existing listIndexedEvidence/listQuarantinedEvidence and fs-safety tests continue to pass.
close_reason: Shipped in commit b2aa052. src/oracle/v18/artifact_index_lock.ts adds serializeArtifactIndexUpdate + upsertArtifactIndexEntry on top of pane 6's read-only evidence.ts. Two layers of protection: (1) in-process per-path async mutex keyed by the resolved+case-normalised file path so concurrent callers for the same indexFile queue in arrival order, (2) atomic on-disk write — write to .tmp.<pid>.<random> in the same directory, fsync the file, rename over target, fsync the parent directory on POSIX. Readers see either the old or new file, never partial. 12 tests cover: fresh creation, sha256 receipt, null-skip, atomic temp+rename, 50 concurrent upserts to the same index lose ZERO entries (explicit regression), 20 concurrent produce a single valid file, upsert dedupes by artifact_id, per-path locks are independent (40 parallel writes split across 2 files both complete), mutator sees fresh index not stale snapshot, async mutators accepted, lock releases on throw (no deadlock), and a serialisation invariant test that asserts maxConcurrent === 1 across 10 interleaved mutators. Module is purely additive — evidence.ts read-only per bead domain — so existing readArtifactIndex/writeArtifactIndex keep working; future callers (incl. writeEvidence) should migrate to these helpers. Existing listIndexedEvidence/listQuarantinedEvidence + fs-safety tests unaffected (399 v18 tests pass via rch exec -- pnpm vitest).

title: Fix typecheck: tests/cli/remote/doctor.test.ts type mismatches
description: rch exec -- pnpm run typecheck reveals 3 errors in tests/cli/remote/doctor.test.ts: (1) line 48: null assignment to 'number | undefined' (TS2322), (2) line 93: 'error' not in type '{ ok: boolean }' (TS2353), (3) line 104: 'statusCode' not in expected return type. Fix the test type assertions to match the actual production types from oracle-94o (e20cf40 commit).
close_reason: done

title: Avoid O(n^2) evidence ledger append cost under serialized sessions
description:
Location: src/oracle/evidence_ledger.ts:188-224 and src/oracle/evidence_ledger.ts:263-309, under the per-ledger serialization in src/oracle/evidence_ledger_concurrency.ts:30-60.

Finding: appendEvidenceLedgerEvent serializes appends per ledger path, but each append calls readEvidenceLedgerInternal(), which reads the entire ledger file, parses every line, and verifies the whole chain before appending one new entry. That makes a session with N ledger milestones do O(N^2) JSON parse/hash work across N appends while all contenders wait on the same session mutex.

Estimated impact: a 64-append concurrency regression is fine today, but long browser/pro sessions that emit hundreds or thousands of milestones will re-read/re-hash the complete ledger hundreds or thousands of times. At 1,000 entries this is roughly 500k entry parses/hash checks before counting lock wait; with multiple panes appending to the same session it directly inflates p95 append latency.

Acceptance: add an append fast path that reads only the tail entry, maintains a compact tail/sequence sidecar, or otherwise avoids whole-chain verification in the append critical section; keep full-chain verification for explicit read/verify commands; add a scaling regression that proves append work is O(1) or near-O(1) per event for a pre-populated ledger.
close_reason: Replaced full-ledger append rescans with stat-validated head cache, batched durable sidecar, and bounded tail-read fallback; added perf regression coverage.

title: Constrain Gemini image downloads before attaching cookies
description:
Security audit finding: src/gemini-web/parse.ts preserves web image URLs from provider output, including https://example.com/img.png in tests, and src/gemini-web/client.ts saveFirstGeminiImageFromOutput falls back to output.images[0] when no generated image exists. downloadGeminiImage then sends the full Google/Gemini cookie header to that URL. Its fetchWithCookiePreservingRedirects also deliberately reuses the same cookie-bearing headers across redirect hops without an origin allowlist.

Acceptance: only cookie-authenticated Gemini image downloads may target explicit Google-owned hosts and paths required for generated images, such as lh3.googleusercontent.com/gg-dl and approved redirect destinations; ordinary web image candidates must not receive Google cookies; redirects are followed manually with per-hop host validation; regression tests cover a web image on example.com and a googleusercontent URL redirecting to an untrusted host.
close_reason: Shipped in 3f80e62: Gemini image downloads now validate lh3.googleusercontent.com/gg-dl before attaching cookies, validate each manual redirect hop against approved generated-image destinations, skip ordinary parsed web images for cookie-authenticated downloads, and cover example.com plus untrusted redirect regressions.

title: Fix typecheck: tests/cli/evidence/ledger_register.test.ts readonly modifier on non-array
description: rch exec -- pnpm run typecheck reveals: tests/cli/evidence/ledger_register.test.ts:20:20 uses 'readonly' on a type that's not an array or tuple literal (TS1354). The fix from oracle-rg2.1 (commit 488bfe3) introduced this. Adjust the type declaration to use ReadonlyArray<T> or remove the readonly keyword.
close_reason: Fixed TS1354 in tests/cli/evidence/ledger_register.test.ts by changing readonly Array<T> to ReadonlyArray<T>. Ran rch exec -- pnpm run typecheck; the ledger_register error is gone, with remaining failures in unrelated files tracked separately by the orchestrator.

title: Fix typecheck: chatgptPro.ts + geminiDeepThink.ts string|undefined assignment
description: rch exec -- pnpm run typecheck: src/cli/commands/run/chatgptPro.ts:421 and src/cli/commands/run/geminiDeepThink.ts:447 both pass 'string | undefined' to a parameter that expects 'string'. Add nullish coalescing or guard. These came from oracle-6ll (169987b) and oracle-w20 (fef3418).
close_reason: done

title: Make evidence ledger head-cache update non-fatal after append
description:
Targeted audit of commit 4cccfb8 found that src/oracle/evidence_ledger.ts appends the ledger line, then awaits recordEvidenceLedgerAppend(filePath, entry). recordEvidenceLedgerAppend performs fs.stat and may flush the non-authoritative head-cache sidecar. If stat/sidecar write/rename fails after fs.appendFile succeeded, appendEvidenceLedgerEvent rejects even though the ledger entry is already durable; callers can retry and duplicate the event or falsely fail a successful browser run.

Acceptance: failures updating the in-memory/durable head cache after a successful append do not reject the append result. Invalidate or drop the cache on update failure and surface at most a warning/diagnostic. Add a regression that injects a failing head-cache stat or flush after append and proves appendEvidenceLedgerEvent resolves and the ledger contains exactly one valid entry.
close_reason: Made head-cache maintenance non-fatal after append and added regression proving flush failures do not corrupt the ledger chain.

title: ChatGPT Pro FSM has no state for mid-run CDP disconnect or reattach
description:
src/browser/state/chatgptPro.ts (oracle-php) models 12 legal forward states + 8 absorbing failure states, but none of them represent mid-run loss of CDP connectivity. Grep for reattach_pending, cdp_disconnect, connection_lost, session_expired returns zero hits in either chatgptPro.ts or geminiDeepThink.ts. AGENTS.md lines 22-23 explicitly name this as a known failure class: 'Long Pro browser runs can outlive local tab/session reachability.'

The consequence: a run that successfully reaches mode_verified_same_session and submits a prompt, then loses the CDP target during the 10m-1h Pro thinking window, has no graceful FSM transition. The output-capture proLongWait controller (oracle-qfl) can produce a needs_reattach decision but the FSM has no event to feed it back through. The driver currently treats the disconnect as an unhandled noop or upgrades it to ui_drift_suspected, which is the wrong v18 error code (the run is still recoverable via 'oracle session <id> --render').

Acceptance: add a 'reattach_pending' legal state between prompt_submitted/response_waiting and output_captured. Add events session_lost (transitions any post-mode-verified state into reattach_pending with the recovery command in failureReason) and reattach_succeeded (transitions back to response_waiting). Add a corresponding remote_browser_unavailable_mid_run failure path for when the budget elapses without reattach. Tests cover the legal reattach cycle, the budget-exhausted path, and that submit_prompt is still rejected after a partial reconnect that has not re-verified the mode. Wire the proLongWait needs_reattach verdict to the new session_lost event so the long-wait controller and FSM agree.
close_reason: Added ChatGPT Pro reattach_pending and remote_browser_unavailable_mid_run FSM states, wired proLongWait needs_reattach into session_lost, and covered CDP disconnect recovery regressions.
